description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone wall base, constructed from irregularly shaped, weathered stones with visible moss or lichen growth. The well is topped by a wooden gabled roof supported by four sturdy wooden posts, providing shelter from the elements. A wooden crank handle is attached to one side, connected to a horizontal spindle for raising and lowering a bucket. The bucket, made of wood with metal bands, rests on the stone rim, ready for use. The roof is made of wooden planks, aged and slightly uneven, adding to the rustic appearance. The interior of the well is open, allowing access to the water below.
description: A rustic stone water well with a wooden gabled roof, crank handle, and bucket, featuring weathered materials and mossy stonework.
description_view_0: The well is seen from the side, highlighting the wooden gabled roof and the stone wall, with the roof's support beams clearly visible.
description_view_1: The front view shows the wooden bucket on the stone rim, the crank handle on the left, and the full width of the wooden roof.
description_view_2: The opposite side view displays the roof structure and stone wall, with the bucket visible on the rim and the crank handle less prominent.
description_view_3: The rear view shows the back of the wooden roof and stone wall, with the crank handle on the right and no bucket visible.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A wooden bucket with metal bands and a crank handle mechanism are present, attached to the side and rim of the well.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: A circular stone wall approximately 1 meter high surrounds the well, with a wooden gabled roof supported by four posts.
